table_description: The buildings table contains information about various buildings, including their location, facility type, and other relevant details. It can be used for analysis and tracking of building-related data.

columns.name: building_eid
columns.description: The building_eid column uniquely identifies records and serves as a primary identifier for each building.

columns.name: building_number
columns.description: The building_number column stores the building number, which can be used to distinguish between multiple buildings in the same area.

columns.name: building_name
columns.description: The building_name column contains the name of the building, providing a descriptive label for each record.

columns.name: building_facility_type
columns.description: The building_facility_type column categorizes the building by its facility type, allowing for analysis of different building types.

columns.name: building_ownership_type
columns.description: The building_ownership_type column indicates the ownership type of the building, which can be used to analyze ownership patterns.

columns.name: building_full_address
columns.description: The building_full_address column stores the complete address of the building, providing location information.

columns.name: city
columns.description: The city column contains the city where the building is located, allowing for geographic analysis.

columns.name: state
columns.description: The state column stores the state where the building is located, providing additional geographic context.

columns.name: zip_code
columns.description: The zip_code column uniquely identifies records and serves as a primary key, referencing the postal code of the building's location.

columns.name: building_latitude
columns.description: The building_latitude column stores the latitude coordinate of the building's location, enabling geographic analysis and mapping.

columns.name: building_longitude
columns.description: The building_longitude column contains the longitude coordinate of the building's location, allowing for precise geographic positioning.

columns.name: active_in
columns.description: The active_in column indicates whether the building is currently active, providing a status flag for analysis.

columns.name: active_units_count
columns.description: The active_units_count column stores the number of active units in the building, allowing for analysis of occupancy rates.

columns.name: unit_count
columns.description: The unit_count column contains the total number of units in the building, providing a measure of building size.

columns.name: is_single_elevator
columns.description: The is_single_elevator column indicates whether the building has a single elevator, which can be used to analyze building infrastructure.

columns.name: geo_type
columns.description: The geo_type column stores the geographic type of the building, although its specific meaning is unclear without additional context.

columns.name: geo_price
columns.description: The geo_price column contains geographic pricing information, although its exact purpose is ambiguous without further context.

columns.name: update_date
columns.description: The update_date column stores the date when the building's information was last updated, allowing for tracking of data freshness.

columns.name: building_row_number
columns.description: The building_row_number column stores a row number for the building, which can be used for internal data processing or sorting purposes.
